spec: Implement `typeOf(v): string`, default-exported from src/typeOf.js, tests in test/typeOf.test.js (node:test + node:assert/strict). Returns a normalized type tag. Acceptance: 1) null -> 'null' (checked BEFORE object); 2) arrays -> 'array' (BEFORE object); 3) 'number','string','boolean','object' for the rest. TDD: write the test file FIRST (one case per tag incl. null and [] ), watch RED, implement to GREEN. No deps. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `deepEqual(a, b): boolean`, default-exported from src/deepEqual.js, tests in test/deepEqual.test.js. Structural equality for JSON-like values. Acceptance: 1) primitives compare by value (1===1, 'a'==='a', null===null); 2) nested objects equal regardless of key order; 3) arrays equal element-wise (order matters); 4) different shapes/lengths -> false. TDD: tests FIRST, RED, then GREEN. May reuse ./typeOf.js. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `deepClone(v): any`, default-exported from src/deepClone.js, tests in test/deepClone.test.js. Deep copy of JSON-like values. Acceptance: 1) mutating the clone (incl. nested) does NOT affect the original; 2) deepEqual(clone, original) is true; 3) primitives returned as-is. TDD: tests FIRST (assert independence after mutation), RED, GREEN. May reuse ./deepEqual.js in tests. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `validateSchema(doc, schema): string[]`, default-exported from src/validateSchema.js, tests in test/validateSchema.test.js. schema maps field->expected type tag (as typeOf returns). Returns an array of human error strings; empty array means valid. Acceptance: 1) all fields correct -> []; 2) a wrong-typed field yields one error string containing the field name; 3) a missing required field yields an error. TDD first, RED, GREEN. Imports ./typeOf.js. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `createCollection(): object`, default-exported from src/createCollection.js, tests in test/createCollection.test.js. Returns a fresh collection object holding internal storage and a next-id counter (e.g. { _docs: [], _nextId: 1 }). Acceptance: 1) two calls return independent collections (mutating one does not affect the other); 2) a new collection has zero documents. TDD first, RED, GREEN. No deps. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `insert(coll, doc): object`, default-exported from src/insert.js, tests in test/insert.test.js. Stores a deepClone of doc with an auto-assigned integer `_id` (1,2,3,...), returns the stored doc (cloned). Acceptance: 1) returned doc has _id===1 for the first insert, 2 for the second; 2) mutating the input doc after insert does NOT change stored data (clone); 3) collection now reports the doc. Imports ./deepClone.js and uses ./createCollection.js shape. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `getById(coll, id): object|null`, default-exported from src/getById.js, tests in test/getById.test.js. Returns a deepClone of the stored doc with that _id, or null. Acceptance: 1) returns the doc after insert; 2) returns null for a missing id; 3) the returned object is a clone (mutating it does not change storage). Uses ./insert.js + ./createCollection.js + ./deepClone.js. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `removeById(coll, id): boolean`, default-exported from src/removeById.js, tests in test/removeById.test.js. Acceptance: 1) removing an existing id returns true and getById then returns null; 2) removing a missing id returns false; 3) other docs are untouched. Uses ./insert.js, ./getById.js. TDD first, RED, GREEN. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `updateById(coll, id, patch): object|null`, default-exported from src/updateById.js, tests in test/updateById.test.js. Shallow-merges patch keys into the stored doc (clone semantics), returns the updated doc clone or null if missing. Acceptance: 1) patched field changes, unpatched fields remain; 2) _id is preserved and cannot be overwritten by patch; 3) missing id -> null. Uses ./insert.js, ./getById.js, ./deepClone.js. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `matchEquals(doc, query): boolean`, default-exported from src/matchEquals.js, tests in test/matchEquals.test.js. True iff for every key in query, deepEqual(doc[key], query[key]). Acceptance: 1) empty query matches any doc; 2) single field equality matches or fails correctly; 3) nested object value matched via deepEqual. Imports ./deepEqual.js. TDD first, RED, GREEN. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `find(coll, query): object[]`, default-exported from src/find.js, tests in test/find.test.js. Returns an array of cloned docs for which matchEquals(doc, query) is true, in insertion order. Acceptance: 1) returns all docs for empty query; 2) filters by an equality field; 3) results are clones (mutation-safe). Uses ./insert.js, ./matchEquals.js, ./deepClone.js. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `matchOperators(value, condition): boolean`, default-exported from src/matchOperators.js, tests in test/matchOperators.test.js. condition is an object of operators: $gt,$gte,$lt,$lte,$ne,$in (array),$nin (array). All present operators must hold. Acceptance: 1) {$gt:5} true for 6 false for 5; 2) {$in:[1,2]} membership; 3) combined {$gte:1,$lte:10} range; 4) unknown operator throws TypeError. TDD first, RED, GREEN. No hard deps. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `matchQuery(doc, query): boolean`, default-exported from src/matchQuery.js, tests in test/matchQuery.test.js. Per field: if the query value is a plain object of operators ($-prefixed keys) use matchOperators(doc[field], cond), else equality via deepEqual. Acceptance: 1) equality field still works; 2) operator field {age:{$gt:18}} works; 3) mixed fields all-must-hold. Imports ./matchOperators.js + ./deepEqual.js. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `matchLogical(doc, query): boolean`, default-exported from src/matchLogical.js, tests in test/matchLogical.test.js. Supports top-level $and (array), $or (array), $not (sub-query); any non-$ keys are delegated to matchQuery. Acceptance: 1) $and requires all; 2) $or requires any; 3) $not negates; 4) bare field query (no logical op) still works via matchQuery. Imports ./matchQuery.js. TDD first, RED, GREEN. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `project(doc, projection): object`, default-exported from src/project.js, tests in test/project.test.js. projection maps field->1 (include) or 0 (exclude); a projection cannot mix include and exclude (throw TypeError if it does), except _id which may always be toggled. Acceptance: 1) include-mode returns only listed fields (+_id unless _id:0); 2) exclude-mode returns all but listed; 3) mixed include/exclude (besides _id) throws. Returns a new object. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `sortDocs(docs, sortSpec): object[]`, default-exported from src/sortDocs.js, tests in test/sortDocs.test.js. sortSpec maps field->1 (asc) or -1 (desc); multi-key supported in object key order; sort is STABLE; returns a new array (input not mutated). Acceptance: 1) single-key asc/desc; 2) multi-key tie-break; 3) stability for equal keys; 4) input array unchanged. TDD first, RED, GREEN. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `limitSkip(docs, opts): object[]`, default-exported from src/limitSkip.js, tests in test/limitSkip.test.js. opts {skip?:number, limit?:number}. Applies skip then limit; missing means no bound; returns a new array. Acceptance: 1) limit caps length; 2) skip drops leading; 3) skip+limit window; 4) negative or non-integer skip/limit throws TypeError. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `query(coll, spec): object[]`, default-exported from src/query.js, tests in test/query.test.js. spec { filter?, project?, sort?, skip?, limit? }. Pipeline order: filter (matchLogical) -> sort (sortDocs) -> skip/limit (limitSkip) -> project (project on each). Returns cloned docs. Acceptance: 1) filter+sort+limit together return the right window; 2) projection applied last; 3) empty spec returns all docs cloned. Imports ./find or ./matchLogical.js, ./sortDocs.js, ./limitSkip.js, ./project.js, ./deepClone.js. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `tokenize(input): object[]`, default-exported from src/tokenize.js, tests in test/tokenize.test.js. Tokenizes a tiny query string. Tokens: identifiers, numbers, string-literals (single quotes), operators (= != > >= < <=), and keywords and/or/not (case-insensitive). Each token = { type, value }. Acceptance: 1) `age > 18` -> 3 tokens [ident,op,number]; 2) `name = 'bob'` parses the string literal; 3) `a > 1 and b < 2` includes an 'and' keyword token; 4) an illegal character throws TypeError. TDD first, RED, GREEN. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `parseExpr(tokens): object`, default-exported from src/parseExpr.js, tests in test/parseExpr.test.js. Consumes tokenize() output and returns a query object compatible with matchLogical: comparisons become {field:{$op:value}} (= -> equality value, > -> {$gt}, etc.), `and`/`or` build {$and:[...]}/{$or:[...]}, `not` builds {$not:...}. Acceptance: 1) `age > 18` -> {age:{$gt:18}}; 2) `name = 'bob'` -> {name:'bob'}; 3) `a > 1 and b < 2` -> {$and:[{a:{$gt:1}},{b:{$lt:2}}]}. Imports ./tokenize.js in tests. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `createIndex(coll, field): object`, default-exported from src/createIndex.js, tests in test/createIndex.test.js. Builds and attaches a simple equality index (a Map from JSON-stringified field value -> array of _ids) under coll._indexes[field], and returns the index. Acceptance: 1) after indexing, the map groups existing docs by that field's value; 2) an absent field groups under a stable key (e.g. undefined bucket); 3) indexing returns the same structure stored on the collection. Uses ./insert.js + ./createCollection.js. TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `transaction(coll, fn): boolean`, default-exported from src/transaction.js, tests in test/transaction.test.js. Snapshots collection storage (deepClone), runs fn(coll); if fn throws, restores the snapshot and returns false; otherwise returns true. Acceptance: 1) a successful fn (e.g. inserts) commits and returns true; 2) a throwing fn rolls back ALL its mutations and returns false; 3) the rolled-back collection deepEquals its pre-transaction state. Imports ./deepClone.js, ./deepEqual.js (tests). TDD first, RED, GREEN. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `serialize(coll): string` and `deserialize(str): object` BOTH default-exported is impossible, so default-export `serialize` from src/serialize.js and NAMED-export `deserialize` from the SAME file. Tests in test/serialize.test.js. serialize -> JSON string capturing docs + nextId; deserialize rebuilds an equivalent collection. Acceptance: 1) round-trip deserialize(serialize(coll)) yields a collection whose docs deepEqual the originals; 2) the restored nextId continues the sequence; 3) restored collection is independent (mutation-safe). Imports ./deepEqual.js (tests). TDD first. Verify: node --test passes.

Implement `queryString(coll, str): object[]`, default-exported from src/queryString.js, tests in test/queryString.test.js. CAPSTONE integration: tokenize(str) -> parseExpr -> use as the filter in query(coll, {filter}). Returns cloned matching docs. Acceptance: 1) `age > 18` returns only docs with age>18; 2) `name = 'bob'` returns bob docs; 3) `age >= 18 and active = true` applies both. Imports ./tokenize.js, ./parseExpr.js, ./query.js. TDD first, RED, GREEN. Verify: node --test passes with the full pipeline.
